cards.title: Qwen3-8B Encodes a Linear 'Value Axis' That Controls Confidence and Backtracking
cards.why_it_matters: This is a direct interpretability result on a widely-used open model, showing that internal representations linearly encode expected goal success — with causal steering implications relevant to alignment, RLHF, and agent design.
cards.summary: Using synthetic in-context RL data, researchers construct a 'value' axis in Qwen3-8B's activations that distinguishes high vs. low verbalized confidence, correct vs. corrupted code, and rollouts with/without backtracking. Steering toward high value suppresses self-correction; steering low induces exploration. DPO shifts internal value of rewarded behaviors, and post-training assigns low value to politically sensitive queries.

cards.title: ContextRL: Contrastive Context-Selection Objective Boosts Agentic and Multimodal LLM Reasoning
cards.why_it_matters: The indirect auxiliary objective — rewarding correct context selection rather than only final answers — is a clean RL training innovation applicable to both code agents and multimodal models, directly relevant to anyone working on RL fine-tuning or grounding.
cards.summary: ContextRL adds a contrastive context-selection objective to GRPO: given a query-answer pair and two similar contexts, the model is rewarded for picking the supporting one. This encourages fine-grained grounding without directly supervising answers. Gains average +2.2% over standard GRPO on 5 long-horizon benchmarks and +1.8% across 12 VQA benchmarks; data-augmentation baselines using the same contrastive data show little improvement, isolating the objective as the source of gains.

cards.title: EPS Derives Exact Posterior Score for Diffusion Models on Linear Inverse Problems
cards.why_it_matters: Exact closed-form posterior scoring without approximate corrections or likelihood gradients is a meaningful theoretical advance in diffusion-based inverse problem solving, with practical speedups relevant to generative modeling and image restoration research.
cards.summary: The authors derive the exact posterior score in closed form for linear Gaussian inverse problems under general Gaussian interpolants, showing posterior sampling reduces to a denoising problem at an operator-dependent shifted pivot under anisotropic noise. The resulting EPS training objective preserves standard denoiser input/output structure and can be trained from scratch or fine-tuned. On five inverse problems across FFHQ and ImageNet, EPS outperforms training-free and training-based baselines while using roughly an order of magnitude fewer denoiser evaluations than gradient-based samplers.

cards.title: Geometric Action Model (GAM) Uses a Split Pretrained GFM for 3D-Aware Robot Manipulation
cards.why_it_matters: Splitting a pretrained geometric foundation model to handle both perception and causal future prediction is an architecturally novel approach to VLA design, directly addressing the 2D-latent-space limitation of current robot policy models.
cards.summary: GAM splits a pretrained geometric foundation model (GFM) at an intermediate layer: shallow layers encode observations, a causal future predictor inserted at the split forecasts future latent tokens conditioned on language, proprioception, and action history, and the remaining GFM blocks decode geometry and actions. This adds language-conditioned temporal world modeling with minimal architectural change while preserving geometric priors. GAM outperforms foundation-model-scale baselines on simulation and real-robot benchmarks in accuracy, robustness, speed, and model size.

cards.title: MetaSyn: 442-Entry Benchmark Exposes Critical Screening Bottleneck in LLM Meta-Analysis Pipelines
cards.why_it_matters: A rigorously structured scientific reasoning benchmark with verified ground truth across retrieval, screening, and synthesis is directly useful for evaluating and improving LLM agents on systematic evidence tasks.
cards.summary: MetaSyn comprises 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio, each pairing a research question with PI/ECO criteria, a 140k-article PubMed retrieval corpus, verified positive studies, and hard topically-similar negatives. Benchmarking 12 pipeline configurations (nine RAG variants plus a protocol-driven agent) shows a retrieval ceiling of 90.9% recall at K=200, yet no system recovers more than 52.7% of ground-truth included literature — pointing to PI/ECO-guided screening as the critical failure point.

cards.title: HABC Separates Viability and Efficiency Critics for Sparse-Reward VLA Fine-Tuning
cards.why_it_matters: Hierarchical advantage decomposition with intervention-aware credit assignment is a practically important technique for anyone fine-tuning robot policies with online RL under sparse binary outcomes and human intervention.
cards.summary: HABC trains separate critic heads for viability (can the task succeed?) and efficiency (how fast?), merging them via a state-adaptive gate that prioritizes viability when success is uncertain and shifts to efficiency once viability is high. Intervention-aware credit assignment restricts outcome labels to segments executed by the current policy. On three contact-rich bimanual real-robot tasks, HABC raises success rates from SFT baselines of 36%, 44%, and 12% to 92%, 88%, and 38% respectively.
